question: How do you mount the lens of a camera when preparing for photography?
content:
1. Remove the rear lens cap and the camera body cap by turning them in the arrow direction.<PIC>
2. Align an EF-S lens with the camera's white EF-S lens mount index, then turn the lens in the arrow direction until it clicks in place. For any lens other than EF-S, align it with the red EF lens index mark instead.<PIC>
3. Set the lens focus mode switch to <AF>; autofocus will not work if it is set to <MF>. Then remove the front lens cap.<PIC>
When attaching the lens, take care to prevent dust from entering the camera through the lens mount.
ret:
"1. Remove the rear lens cap and the camera body cap by turning them in the arrow direction.<PIC>
2. Align an EF-S lens with the camera's white EF-S lens mount index, then turn the lens in the arrow direction until it clicks in place. For any lens other than EF-S, align it with the red EF lens index mark instead.<PIC>
3. Set the lens focus mode switch to <AF>; autofocus will not work if it is set to <MF>. Then remove the front lens cap.<PIC>
When attaching the lens, take care to prevent dust from entering the camera through the lens mount.",["Manual10_21", "Manual10_22", "Manual10_23"]

evidence_refs.summary: Mounting a lens: remove caps, align EF-S with the white EF-S lens mount index or other lenses with the red EF lens index mark, turn until it clicks, set focus mode switch to AF, and remove the front lens cap.
When attaching or detaching the lens, prevent dust from entering through the lens mount.
Shows removing the rear lens cap and camera body cap.
Shows aligning the lens mount index and turning the lens into place.
Shows the AF/MF focus mode switch and front lens cap removal.

notes: Answered from the Canon EOS 20D Mounting a Lens section. Three images are included because each directly illustrates one mounting step.
